chief_complaint: A 48-year-old man presented with acute focal deficits following a short prodrome of headache and fever.
history: A 48-year-old man with well-controlled HIV presented with acute focal deficits following a short prodrome of headache and fever. He had no conventional vascular risk factors.
exam: Neurological examination revealed right-sided weakness (MRC grade 2/5) and NIHSS score of 11.
labs: Initial laboratory testing and CT imaging were unrevealing. MRI suggested a vasculitic process, and cerebrospinal fluid studies confirmed neurosyphilis.
